summary: A superb soundboard recording of the historic Nassau ’90 show – Birdsong and a jaw‑dropping Eyes of the World make this a must‑listen.
review: The SBD captures an electrifying night where Branford Marsalis’s saxophone lifts the band into jazzy, exploratory territory.  Highlights include a soaring Birdsong, a blistering Eyes of the World, and a second set that many fans describe as “atomic.”  The mix is crystal‑clear with every instrument in its place, making this one of the most celebrated recordings of the era.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive 5‑star commentary praising both performance and soundboard quality; only a single neutral/negative note about a brief Dark Star slip.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners repeatedly call the tape “crisp,” “clear,” and praise the soundboard mix; no significant drop‑outs or muffiness reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set strong with a standout Jack Straw opening; second set described as “atomic,” featuring seamless interplay with Branford and extended jams that eclipse the first half.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring tone, inventive midi‑flute effect, and tight interplay with Branford (only a brief Dark Star slip noted).
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as “everywhere in the mix,” solid bass foundation throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Backup vocals from Mydland highlighted as exceptional; vocal performance strong overall.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s jazzy piano/organ work praised for fitting the guest‑jam vibe; keyboards sit perfectly in the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill/Mickey noted as tight, perfect rhythm section that drives the jams.
